channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Building Economic Stability as the Foundation for Growth
item.description: The article highlights the importance of viewing stability not as a final goal but as the groundwork for sustainable economic growth. Standard Bank emphasises that long-term development in South Afric...

item.title: Heavy rains trigger landslide in Elgeyo Marakwet killing two
item.description: In Elgeyo Marakwet, a tragic landslide occurred on Sunday evening in Kibendo village, Keiyo North sub-county, following continuous heavy rains. Acting County Commissioner Julius Maiyo confirmed that a...

item.title: Johannesburg Cracks Down on Illegal Developers Following Ormonde Building Collapse
item.description: The City of Johannesburg is taking decisive action following the collapse of an illegally constructed building in Ormonde, which claimed the lives of nine construction workers. Preliminary investigati...

item.title: How Democratic Senate primaries are being reshaped by candidates’ positions on Israel and Gaza
item.description: The article examines how Democratic candidates’ relationships with Israel, and their responses to the Gaza war, have become central fault lines in high‑profile primary contests across the United State...

item.title: LSK criticises KMJA over Maua court demonstrations
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) – Meru Chapter has publicly criticised the Kenya Magistrates and Judges Association (KMJA) after the latter condemned protests by lawyers at the Maua Law Courts. KMJA ac...

item.title: Pakistan maintains stable fuel stocks amid regional tensions
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb has assured that Pakistan currently does not face any fuel shortage, despite the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran causing global uncerta...

item.title: India lacks national registry to track 6 lakh children born with birth anomalies annually
item.description: Every year, approximately six lakh babies in India are born with congenital anomalies, yet the country does not have a centralised national registry to track these cases. As infectious diseases declin...

item.title: Asian Tech Stocks Plunge Amid Oil Price Concerns as Europe Stabilizes
item.description: Asian stock markets faced heavy losses on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, as fears of a widening Middle East conflict triggered concerns over rising oil prices. South Korea’s KOSPI benchmark suffered a hist...

item.title: Northern Queensland braces for potential tropical cyclone after heavy rainfall
item.description: Queensland's far north, already drenched by persistent heavy rainfall, is preparing for a possible tropical cyclone expected to hit near Cairns in the coming days. A tropical low northeast of Cooktown...

item.title: Trailer Release: Tamil Nadu Woman’s Journey to Korea in Ra Karthik’s Film
item.description: The trailer for Ra Karthik’s upcoming film 'Made in Korea' has been released, giving audiences a glimpse into the story of a young woman from Tamil Nadu who journeys all the way to South Korea driven ...

item.title: COSATU raises alarm over repeated safety failures in South African construction
item.description: The Congress of South African Trade Unions (COSATU) has expressed serious concern over what it calls growing lawlessness in the construction sector. This follows the tragic collapse of a building in O...

item.title: Justice Mandokhail argues against SC ruling on reserved seats reclassification
item.description: Justice Jamal Khan Mandokhail has released a dissenting note regarding the Supreme Court’s recent ruling on 41 candidates who were reclassified from their original political affiliation to independent...

item.title: Mali Journalist Detained Over Article Challenging Niger President's Claims
item.description: For Mali, gbe body! The military junta don arrest popular journalist Youssouf Sissoko under cybercrime law after e publish article for L'Alternance on 2 February. The write-up dey challenge the talk w...

item.title: Anthropic and the Ethical Boundaries of AI in Military and Surveillance Applications
item.description: In March 2026, Anthropic, a leading AI company, refused to sign a Pentagon contract granting the U.S. military unrestricted access to its AI technology for all lawful purposes. CEO Dario Amodei set tw...

item.title: Comelec clarifies Bong Acop not disqualified in Antipolo special election
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) clarified that reports circulating on social media claiming that Bong Acop, son of the late congressman Romeo Acop, has been disqualified from the upcoming specia...

item.title: Congress’s Struggle to Limit Presidential War Powers Over Time
item.description: Article 1 of the U.S. Constitution grants Congress the authority to declare war, yet modern presidents frequently claim Article 2 allows them to act militarily without prior congressional approval. Ov...

item.title: The rise of high-end bakeries in the UK and the appeal of premium pastries
item.description: In recent years, the UK has witnessed a remarkable surge in luxury bakeries, even amid a cost of living crisis. Independent bakeries have grown by 34% in the past five years, with towns in the Midland...

item.title: Senator Natasha Rolls Out Electric Vehicles to Empower Kogi Central Residents
item.description: Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan don launch one fleet of electric vehicles for Kogi Central as part of her empowerment programme wey dey focused on sustainable transportation and economic development. ...

item.title: Malacañang Urges Swift House Approval of Bill Moving BARMM Polls to September 2026
item.description: Malacañang has called on the House of Representatives to immediately pass its counterpart bill resetting the first regular parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindana...

item.title: Busia police destroy seized bhang and heroin valued at Sh21 million
item.description: Police in Busia County, working alongside the Judiciary, have destroyed a large consignment of bhang and heroin worth over Sh21 million. The drugs were originally seized in 2023 at the Suo roadblock a...
